subject: OpenTelemetry Weaver 0.24.2
note: Verifies CLI behavior and exit codes on fixed registry fixtures, not timing. No statistical distribution applies: for a pinned weaver version each command's exit code and diagnostic are deterministic. source_of_truth = evidence.txt (exit-code matrix) + diff.json.

exp_A_builtin_check.claim: `weaver registry check` gates schema + resolution correctness, but NOT naming style.

cases.note: KEY finding: naming style is NOT caught by the built-in check.

cases.diagnostic: 'not deprecated and does not contain a brief field'.
type must be one of boolean/int/double/string/any/string[]/int[]/double[]/boolean[] (or template[...]).

exp_A2_rego_policy.claim: A layered Rego policy (after_resolution) turns naming into an enforced contract that the built-in check ignores.
exp_A2_rego_policy.takeaway: The SAME registry passes or fails depending only on whether the policy is layered on. 'What is a contract' is a policy choice, not a built-in default.

exp_B_diff.claim: `weaver registry diff` surfaces structural breaking changes as add/remove, but does NOT flag every field-level change; it is informational (exit 0), not a gate.
exp_B_diff.head: reg2/ (id renamed to identifier, total_amount double -> int)
honest_limits: A rename shows up as remove + add — i.e. downstream consumers (dashboards, alerts) keyed on commerce.order.id silently break.

The in-place total_amount double -> int type change is NOT surfaced in the diff summary. Field-level type/stability enforcement lives in the comparison_after_resolution policy layer, not in the default diff.
diff exits 0 (informational). It does not fail CI by itself.

exp_C_generate.claim: One registry source drives generated artifacts (docs/code/SDK) via `weaver registry generate` + Jinja2 templates.
exp_C_generate.note: `weaver registry resolve` is marked deprecated in help and prints a deprecation warning at runtime, but STILL resolves and exits 0 (it does not halt). Use generate/package going forward.

cited_not_measured.claim: weaver 0.24.2 is the latest (2026-06-23); 0.24.0 added requirement_level v2, dependency_resolution.exclude, one_of/all_of, authenticated HTTP, weaver-live-check GH Action.
Registry dependency chains a->b->c up to depth 10 are supported.
cited_not_measured.source: release notes (not exercised in this run)
